title: Adorium divaricatum
taxonomy: Adorium divaricatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. It is native to New Zealand, where it grows in the wild. The exact classification of Adorium divaricatum includes its classification as a member of the order Myrtales, and the family Melastomataceae. The genus Adorium is part of the family Melastomataceae, and its species, including Adorium divaricatum, are recognized as part of this broader classification. Adorium divaricatum is considered to be a unique and endemic species within this classification.
uses: Adorium divaricatum has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
